name: galileo-core

metadata.name: galileo-core
metadata.summary: Shared schemas and configuration for Galileo's Python packages.

documentation:
# galileo-core

Shared schemas and configuration for Galileo's Python packages.

classes.name: galileo_core.exceptions.http.GalileoHTTPException
documentation: Galileo HTTP exception to wrap all http exceptions.

modules.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config
documentation: mypy: disable-error-code=syntax
We need to ignore syntax errors until https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/17535 is resolved.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.set_skip_ssl_validation
documentation: Set the skip SSL validation flag.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.set_log_level
documentation:
Set the log level for the logger.

We allow setting the log level using the string representation of the log level
or the integer representation of the log level.

By default, we set the log level to `WARNING`.

Parameters
----------
value : Optional[Union[int, str]]
    Log level to set.

Returns
-------
int
    Log level set.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.ensure_https_console_url
documentation:
Ensure that the console URL is an HTTPS URL.

Parameters
----------
value : str
    Console URL to validate.

Returns
-------
str
    Validated console URL.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.set_api_url
documentation:
Set the API URL if it's not already set.

We can set the API URL in the following ways:
1. If the API URL is provided, use it.
2. If the console URL matches `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`, use `http://localhost:8088`.
3. If the API URL is not provided, use the console URL to generate the API URL
by replacing `console` with `api`.

Once we determine the API URL, we make a request to the healthcheck endpoint to
validate that it is reachable.

Parameters
----------
api_url : Optional[Union[str, Url]]
    API URL to use. If not provided, we generate it from the console URL.
info : ValidationInfo
    Pydantic validation info object.

Returns
-------
Url
    API URL to use.

Raises
------
ValueError
    If the console URL is not set.
ValueError
    If the API URL can't be generated.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.get_token_from_ui
documentation:
Given a console URL, open the token generation page in the browser and prompt the user to enter the token
generated.

Parameters
----------
console_url : HttpUrl
    Console URL to use.

Returns
-------
str
    JWT token generated by the user.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.get_jwt_token
documentation:
Get the JWT token for the user.

1. If an API key is provided, log in with the API key.
2. If a username and password are provided, log in with the username and password.
3. If no credentials are provided, attempt to log in with a token from the UI.

Parameters
----------
console_url : Optional[HttpUrl], optional
    Console URL, by default None
api_url : Optional[HttpUrl], optional
    API URL, by default None
api_key : Optional[SecretStr], optional
    API key, by default None
username : Optional[str], optional
    Username, by default None
password : Optional[SecretStr], optional
    Password, by default None

Returns
-------
SecretStr
    JWT token for the user, if successful, as a secret string.

Raises
------
AssertionError
    If the console URL is not provided.
AssertionError
    If the API URL is not provided.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.set_jwt_token
documentation:
Set the JWT token for the user.

Parameters
----------
value : Optional[str]
    JWT token to set for the user.
info : ValidationInfo
    Pydantic validation info object.

Returns
-------
SecretStr
    JWT token as a secret string.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.set_validated_api_client
documentation:
Set the validated API client.

This method sets an API client with the validated API URL and JWT token. As a
part of the validation process, we make a request to get the current user's email
address to confirm that the user is logged in successfully.

Parameters
----------
validated_api_client : Optional[ApiClient]
    API client to set.
info : ValidationInfo
    Pydantic validation info object.

Returns
-------
ApiClient
    Validated API client.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.serialize_secrets
documentation: Serialize secret strings to their secret values.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.write
documentation:
Write the config object to a file.

This is only used as a backup for debugging and never read from to set current values.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.refresh_jwt_token
documentation: Refresh token if not present or expired.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.api_client
documentation:
Get the API client.

We refresh the JWT token if it's expired and set the JWT token on the API client
if it's different from the one set on the API client during the config
initialization.

Returns
-------
ApiClient
    Validated API client.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.get
documentation:
Get the current config object.

We try to load the existing global config and return it. If it's not set already, we set the config object from
the environment variables.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.set
documentation: Set the config object from keyword arguments.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.reset
documentation:
Reset the credentials stored in the config object.

Sub-classes can extend this method to reset additional fields.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.base_config.BaseConfig.logout
documentation: Logout the user by resetting the credentials and printing a message.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.step.BaseStep.serialize_to_str
documentation: We disable the check_fields for this serializer because we want to use the same method for `tools`, which is not
a field in the this model but only on the `LlmStep` which is a subclass.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.step.StepWithChildren.add_llm
documentation:
Add a new llm step to the current workflow.

Parameters:
----------
    input: LlmStepAllowedIOType: Input to the node.
    output: LlmStepAllowedIOType: Output of the node.
    model: str: Model used for this step.
    tools: Optional[Sequence[Dict[str, Any]]]: List of available tools passed to LLM on invocation.
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the step.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: duration_ns of the node in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the step's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this step.
    input_tokens: Optional[int]: Number of input tokens.
    output_tokens: Optional[int]: Number of output tokens.
    total_tokens: Optional[int]: Total number of tokens.
    temperature: Optional[float]: Temperature used for generation.
    status_code: Optional[int]: Status code of the node execution.
Returns:
-------
    LlmStep: The created step.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.step.StepWithChildren.add_retriever
documentation:
Add a new retriever step to the current workflow.

Parameters:
----------
    input: StepIOType: Input to the node.
    documents: Union[List[str], List[Dict[str, str]], List[Document]]: Documents retrieved from the retriever.
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the step.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: duration_ns of the node in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the step's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this step.
    status_code: Optional[int]: Status code of the node execution.
Returns:
-------
    RetrieverStep: The created step.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.step.StepWithChildren.add_tool
documentation:
Add a new tool step to the current workflow.

Parameters:
----------
    input: StepIOType: Input to the node.
    output: StepIOType: Output of the node.
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the step.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: duration_ns of the node in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the step's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this step.
    status_code: Optional[int]: Status code of the node execution.
Returns:
-------
    ToolStep: The created step.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.step.StepWithChildren.add_protect
documentation:
Add a new protect step to the current workflow.

Parameters:
----------
    payload: Payload: Input to Protect `invoke`.
    response: Response: Output from Protect `invoke`.
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the step.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: duration_ns of the node in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the step's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this step.
    status_code: Optional[int]: Status code of the node execution.
Returns:
-------
    ToolStep: The created step.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.step.StepWithChildren.add_sub_workflow
documentation:
Add a nested workflow step to the workflow. This is useful when you want to create a nested workflow within the
current workflow. The next step you add will be a child of this workflow. To step out of the nested workflow,
use conclude_workflow().

Parameters:
----------
    input: StepIOType: Input to the node.
    output: Optional[StepIOType]: Output of the node. This can also be set on conclude_workflow().
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the step.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: duration_ns of the node in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the step's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this step.
Returns:
-------
    WorkflowStep: The created step.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.step.StepWithChildren.add_sub_agent
documentation:
Add a nested agent workflow step to the workflow. This is useful when you want to create a nested workflow
within the current workflow. The next step you add will be a child of this workflow. To step out of the nested
workflow, use conclude_workflow().

Parameters:
----------
    input: StepIOType: Input to the node.
    output: Optional[StepIOType]: Output of the node. This can also be set on conclude_workflow().
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the step.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: duration_ns of the node in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the step's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this step.
Returns:
-------
    AgentStep: The created step.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.step.StepWithChildren.conclude
documentation:
Conclude the workflow by setting the output of the current node. In the case of nested workflows, this will
point the workflow back to the parent of the current workflow.

Parameters:
----------
    output: Optional[StepIOType]: Output of the node.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: duration_ns of the node in nanoseconds.
    status_code: Optional[int]: Status code of the node execution.
Returns:
-------
    Optional[StepWithChildren]: The parent of the current workflow. None if no parent exists.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.workflow.Workflows.add_workflow
documentation:
Create a new workflow and add it to the list of workflows.
Simple usage:
```
my_workflows.add_workflow("input")
my_workflows.add_llm_step("input", "output", model="<my_model>")
my_workflows.conclude_workflow("output")
```
Parameters:
----------
    input: StepIOType: Input to the node.
    output: Optional[str]: Output of the node.
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the workflow.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: Duration of the workflow in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the workflow's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this workflow.
    ground_truth: Optional[str]: Ground truth, expected output of the workflow.
Returns:
-------
    WorkflowStep: The created workflow.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.workflow.Workflows.add_agent_workflow
documentation:
Create a new workflow and add it to the list of workflows.
Simple usage:
```
my_workflows.add_agent_workflow("input")
my_workflows.add_tool_step("input", "output")
my_workflows.conclude_workflow("output")
Parameters:
----------
    input: StepIOType: Input to the node.
    output: Optional[str]: Output of the node.
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the workflow.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: Duration of the workflow in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the workflow's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this workflow.
    ground_truth: Optional[str] = None, Ground truth, expected output of the workflow.
Returns:
-------
    AgentStep: The created agent workflow.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.workflow.Workflows.add_single_step_workflow
documentation:
Create a new single-step workflow and add it to the list of workflows. This is just if you need a plain llm
workflow with no surrounding steps.

Parameters:
----------
    input: LlmStepAllowedIOType: Input to the node.
    output: LlmStepAllowedIOType: Output of the node.
    model: str: Model used for this step. Feedback from April: Good docs about what model names we use.
    tools: Optional[List[Dict]]: List of available tools passed to LLM on invocation.
    name: Optional[str]: Name of the step.
    duration_ns: Optional[int]: duration_ns of the node in nanoseconds.
    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the step's creation.
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Metadata associated with this step.
    input_tokens: Optional[int]: Number of input tokens.
    output_tokens: Optional[int]: Number of output tokens.
    total_tokens: Optional[int]: Total number of tokens.
    temperature: Optional[float]: Temperature used for generation.
    ground_truth: Optional[str]: Ground truth, expected output of the workflow.
    status_code: Optional[int]: Status code of the node execution.
Returns:
-------
    LlmStep: The created step.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.shared.workflows.workflow.Workflows.add_llm_step
documentation:
Add a new llm step to the current workflow.

Parameters:

Parameters:

documentation:
Add a nested workflow step to the workflow. This is useful when you want to create a nested workflow within the

    created_at_ns: Optional[int]: Timestamp of the step's creation.

Returns:
-------

documentation:
Add a nested agent workflow step to the workflow. This is useful when you want to create a nested workflow

Parameters:

    output: Optional[StepIOType]: Output of the node. This can also be set on conclude_workflow().

classes.name: galileo_core.schemas.protect.execution_status.ExecutionStatus
documentation: Status of the execution.

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.protect.subscription_config.SubscriptionConfig.possible_statuses
documentation:
List of possible statuses that will trigger a notification to be sent.

This is a list of the cased and uncased versions of the statuses in the `statuses` list.

Returns
-------
List[str]
    _description_

methods.name: galileo_core.schemas.protect.request.Request.validate_project_stage
documentation:
Validate that one of:
1. stage_id
2. stage_name and project_id
3. stage_name and project_name
are provided.

Returns
-------
Request
    The request object.

Raises
------
ValueError
    If the validation fails.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.pagination.paginated_request
documentation:
Get results from a request that may be paginated.

We make an API request (from a partial function) and get the results from the response. If the response is paginated,
we make a follow-up request with the next starting token and append the results to the list.

Parameters
----------
request_partial : Callable[..., Dict]
    Partial function for making an API request.
result_key : str
    Key in the response JSON to get the results from.
starting_token : int, optional
    Starting token for the request, by default 0
limit : int, optional
    Limit for the number of items to request, by default 25

Returns
-------
List[Any]
    List of results from the API request.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.dependencies.is_dependency_available
documentation:
Check if a dependency is available.

Parameters
----------
name : str
    The name of the dependency to check.

Returns
-------
bool
    True if the dependency is available, False otherwise.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.user.get_current_user
documentation:
Get the current user.

Returns
-------
User
    Current user.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.user.invite_users
documentation:
Invite users.

Parameters
----------
emails : List[str]
    List of emails to invite.
role : UserRole, optional
    Roles to grant invited users, by default UserRole.user
group_ids : Optional[List[UUID4]], optional
    Group IDs to add the users to, by default None, which means they are not added to any group.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.user.list_users
documentation:
List all users.

Returns
-------
List[User]
    List of all users.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.user.update_user
documentation:
Update user.

Parameters
----------
user_id : User.id
    User ID to update.
role : UserRole
    New role to assign to the user.

Returns
-------
User
    Updated user.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.group.create_group
documentation:
Create a group.

Parameters
----------
name : str
    Name of the group.
description : Optional[str], optional
    Description for the group, by default None
visibility : GroupVisibility, optional
    Visiblity of the group, by default GroupVisibility.public

Returns
-------
CreateGroupResponse
    Response object for the created group.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.group.list_groups
documentation:
List all groups for the user.

Returns
-------
List[CreateGroupResponse]
    List of all groups.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.group.add_users_to_group
documentation:
Add users to a an existing group with the specified role.

Parameters
----------
group_id : UUID4
    Group ID.
user_ids : List[UUID4]
    List of user IDs to add to the group.
role : GroupRole
    Role of the user in the group, by default GroupRole.member.

Returns
-------
List[AddGroupMemberResponse]
    List of responses for each user added to the group.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.project.create_project
documentation:
Create a project.

Parameters
----------
request : CreateProjectRequest
    Request object for creating a project.

Returns
-------
ProjectResponse
    Response object for the created project.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.project.get_project
documentation:
Get a project by either ID or name.

If both project_id and project_name are provided, project_id will take precedence.

For cases when the project name is if the project_type is provided, it will be used
to filter the projects by type if the project_name is provided. If raise_if_missing
is True, a ValueError will be raised if the project is not found. This is useful
when the project is expected to exist, and the absence of the project would be an error.

Parameters
----------
project_id : Optional[UUID4], optional
    Project ID, by default None.
project_name : Optional[str], optional
    Project name, by default None.
project_type : Optional[ProjectType], optional
    Project type, by default None.
raise_if_missing : bool, optional
    Raise an error if the project is not found, by default True.

Returns
-------
Optional[ProjectResponse]
    Project response object if the project is found, None otherwise.

Raises
------
ValueError
    If neither project_id nor project_name is provided.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.project.get_project_from_id
documentation:
Given a project ID, get the project.

Parameters
----------
project_id : UUID4
    Project ID.

Returns
-------
ProjectResponse
    Project response object.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.project.get_projects
documentation:
Given a project type, returns all projects of that type that the user has access to.

Parameters
----------
project_type : ProjectType
    The type of projects to get.

Returns
-------
List[ProjectResponse]
    A list of projects of the given type.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.project.get_project_from_name
documentation:
Get a project by name.

Parameters
----------
project_name : str
    Name of the project.
project_type : Optional[ProjectType], optional
    Type of the project to filter by, by default None.
raise_if_missing : bool, optional
    Raise an error if the proejct is not found, by default True.

Returns
-------
Optional[ProjectResponse]
    Project response object if the project is found, None otherwise.

Raises
------
ValueError
    If the project is not found and raise_if_missing is True.

functions.name: galileo_core.helpers.execution.async_run
documentation:
Run an async coroutine synchronously.

This function is useful for running async code in a synchronous context. This will
ensure that the async code is run in a separate thread and the result is returned
to the caller.

Parameters
----------
coroutine : Coroutine
    The coroutine to run.

Returns
-------
Any
    The result of the coroutine.

methods.name: galileo_core.helpers.execution.AsyncExecutor.__enter__
documentation: Copied from: https://gist.github.com/gsakkis/18bc444607a590fe3f084a77aa54b4c2
